text.title: gha-deployment-patterns Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for gha-deployment-patterns. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Production deploy jobs MUST use a GitHub Environment with required reviewers. Set cancel-in-progress: false on deploy concurrency groups so in-flight production deploys are never cancelled by a new commit.
rule.rationale: cancel-in-progress: true on a production deploy group kills an active rollout when a new commit is pushed — a partial deploy is worse than a delayed one.

rule.statement: Reusable workflows MUST pass only the secrets they need, not secrets: inherit. Each secret must be listed explicitly in the secrets: block with a description.
rule.rationale: secrets: inherit passes every secret in the calling repo's scope. Over-exposure of prod secrets to staging workflows violates least privilege and creates blast radius if the workflow is compromised.

rule.statement: Every job in a CD workflow MUST declare timeout-minutes. Use 15-30 for tests, 60 for builds, 120 for deploys. The default 360-minute timeout is too long and leaves runners occupied during an outage.
rule.rationale: A hung Kubernetes rollout will silently occupy a runner for 6 hours without a timeout. Explicit timeouts surface stuck deployments early and free runners for other work.

rule.statement: Reusable workflows MUST define outputs for any value downstream workflows need (e.g., deployment-url). Producing steps MUST have id: and the job MUST have an outputs: mapping.
rule.rationale: Agents commonly miss one half of the output chain: a step with id: but no job-level outputs: produces empty string for consumers, causing silent failures.
